paper_focus.title: The influence of silicon and aluminum alloying on the lattice parameter and stacking fault energy of austenitic steel
paper_focus.research_objective: To determine the effects of Si and Al alloying on the austenite lattice parameter and stacking fault energy, and to evaluate their implications for carbon content estimation and austenite stability in advanced high-strength steels.
main_process_variables: Solution annealing temperatures: Base 1040 °C, 2.5 Si 1075 °C, 2.5 Al 1060 °C
5% compression true strain to introduce dislocations
XRD sample preparation: grinding to 600 grit, chemical polishing
TEM sample preparation: electropolishing with 95% acetic acid and 5% perchloric acid

visual_evidence.description: This weak-beam dark-field (WBDF) transmission electron micrograph displays a dissociated dislocation in the base Fe-Ni-Cr austenitic steel (Fe-15Ni-11Cr base composition), serving as the representative imaging condition...

Figure 2 presents a scatter plot illustrating the dependence of the austenite lattice parameter on silicon and aluminum alloying in Fe-Ni-Cr-based austenitic steels. The y-axis displays the Austenite Lattice Parameter...

Figure 3 comprises two scatter plots illustrating the influence of silicon and aluminum alloying on stacking fault energy (SFE) in Fe-Ni-Cr austenitic steels. Subfigure (a) displays partial dislocation separation...
